2603.30031.title: Cognitive Friction: A Decision-Theoretic Framework for Bounded Deliberation in Tool-Using Agents
2603.30031.paper_summary: Autonomous tool-using agents operating in networked environments must decide which information source to query and when to stop querying and act. Without principled bounds on information-acquisition costs, unconstrained agents exhibit systematic failure modes: excessive tool use under congestion, prolonged deliberation under time decay, and brittle behavior under ambiguous evidence. We propose the Triadic Cognitive Architecture (TCA), a unified decision-theoretic framework that formalizes these failure modes through the concept of Cognitive Friction. By synthesizing nonlinear filtering theory, congestion-dependent cost dynamics, and HJB optimal stopping, we model deliberation as a stochastic control problem over a joint belief-congestion state space, where information acquisition is explicitly priced by tool-dependent signal quality and live network load. Rather than relying on arbitrary heuristic stop-tokens or fixed query budgets, TCA derives an HJB-inspired stopping boundary and instantiates a computable rollout-based approximation of belief-dependent value-of-information with a net-utility halting condition. We validate the framework on two controlled simulation environments, the Emergency Medical Diagnostic Grid (EMDG) and the Network Security Triage Grid (NSTG), designed to isolate key decision-theoretic quantities under reproducible conditions. TCA reduces time-to-action while improving resource outcomes without degrading accuracy: over greedy baselines, TCA gains 36 viability points in EMDG and 33 integrity points in NSTG. Ablations confirm joint optimization of selection and stopping is essential; stopping rules alone recover at most 4 viability points. A sensitivity sweep over alpha, beta, lambda_S shows stable accuracy and interpretable tradeoffs; an empirical sweep over eta in {0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5} confirms eta=0 is optimal on EMDG trajectories under high temporal urgency.
2603.30031.paper_summary_zh: 自主工具使用代理在網絡環境中運作時，必須決定查詢哪個信息來源以及何時停止查詢並行動。<lb/>如果沒有對信息獲取成本的原則性界限，無約束的代理會出現系統性失敗模式：在擁堵下過度使用工具、在時間衰減下長時間思考，以及在模糊證據下出現脆弱行為。<lb/>我們提出了三元認知架構（TCA），這是一個統一的決策理論框架，通過認知摩擦的概念形式化這些失敗模式。<lb/>通過綜合非線性過濾理論、依賴擁堵的成本動態和HJB最優停止，我們將思考建模為一個在聯合信念-擁堵狀態空間上的隨機控制問題，其中信息獲取的成本明確地由工具依賴的信號質量和實時網絡負載定價。<lb/>TCA並不依賴於任意的啟發式停止標記或固定的查詢預算，而是推導出一個受HJB啟發的停止邊界，並實現了一個可計算的基於滾動的信念依賴的信息價值近似，並具有淨效用停止條件。<lb/>我們在兩個受控的模擬環境中驗證了該框架，即緊急醫療診斷網格（EMDG）和網絡安全分流網格（NSTG），這些環境旨在在可重複的條件下隔離關鍵的決策理論量。<lb/>TCA減少了行動時間，同時改善了資源結果而不降低準確性：相較於貪婪基線，TCA在EMDG中獲得了36個可行性點，在NSTG中獲得了33個完整性點。<lb/>消融實驗確認選擇和停止的聯合優化是必不可少的；僅僅停止規則最多只能恢復4個可行性點。<lb/>對alpha、beta、lambda_S的敏感性掃描顯示穩定的準確性和可解釋的權衡；對{0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5}中eta的實證掃描確認在高時間緊迫性下，eta=0在EMDG軌跡上是最佳的。
2603.30031.author: Davide Di Gioia et.al.
